HELP FOR A FRIEND

Yes, it's really for a friend. We've maintained a friendship for about 14 years, and our dynamic is more like siblings—arguing but always reconciling—or "frenemies," which feels more accurate.

In our twenties, I was married while he faced various setbacks: the painful loss of a friend, a justified dismissal from a well-paying job, and understood and rightfully breakup on the side of his girl

His family: poisoned by the envy of his sisters, lukewarm affection mixed with distrust from his parents, and low self-esteem. When I called his sister to discuss him, her response was cold: "What did he do this time?" This lack of concern indicates a deeper issue within the family dynamic.

**CHILDHOOD STRUGGLES**

*Crushed by Bullying:* Growing up, he was overshadowed by better-looking boys, receiving less pride and attention from parents compared to his sisters. He struggled to find his worth.

*Hatred Toward Women:* The cold affection from his mother and mockery from his sisters led to a toxic relationship with women, marked by need and fear. He expressed a "reasonable" interest in people of the same sex.

**THE BAD**

*Vindictive Determination:* He aimed to escape his small town and become a wealthy professional, eventually succeeding in Europe but at a significant personal cost.

*Dazzlement:* City life transformed his mindset but led to serious mistakes, resulting in job loss and sudden poverty.

*Unlearned Lessons:* He continued inappropriate online behavior and engaged in shady relationships while dating his girlfriend.

*The Rose:* He viewed his girlfriend as "The Rose" from *The Little Prince*, trying to control her. After their breakup, he attempted to stalk her repeatedly.

*Scary role models:* He defines *Sylar,* from *Heroes* (a villain who murders his mother) and *Dexter,* the serial killer who kills serial killers as 'admirable and cool'.

*Cruel Hobbies:* Online harassment, stalking and researching, threatening, and bullying people for fun. Once he was identified, he delved deeper into this twisted world. 

**THE UGLY**

*Socially Functional Marriage:* He is unattracted to his wife, spending excessive time admiring other women online. However, her social advantages contribute to his self-promoted image of success.

*Spiral of Bitterness:* Despite material success, he frequently expresses melancholy through his words and photos, displaying a vague, depressed demeanor.

*Delusions of Grandeur:* He proposed creating a fundamentalist religious union, making others uncomfortable with his extremist views.

*Love for Shadows:* He confessed to stalking a member of the initial group he harassed online, feeling compelled to destroy him.

*Unlearned Lessons 2:* He continues to conduct inappropriate relationships through various social media profiles and remains obsessed with disturbing graphic content.

*Explosive Hostility:* He has threatened me multiple times, indicating he has plans for those he despises. He talks about "paying back" those who caused him pain.

Despite moments of virtue and appearing to be a good family man, it’s as if his conscience floats and sinks, revealing the storm within his personality. I seek your guidance on how to approach his family and address these urgent issues
Cause: trauma( childhood bullying, family dynamics, neglect)

Am I Having Hallucinations?

Ok, so to start off, I have been diagnosed and treated before for a number of mental disorders, including Bipolar Disorder, chronic anxiety, insomnia, major depressive disorder, BPD, etc., all inherited from my father, although BPD is on my mother's side. I havent taken my medication or had treatment of any kind for about 3 months now. I so also have autism and extremly impaired social skills and lower emotional maturity than most people, although I tend to absorb information very easily and have been told I have high above average intelligence by many doctors before in the past. I also have an IQ of 127.

Anyways, so that you guys can understand what kind of mental state i am in currently, I currently just had a falling out with my best friend, I barely get enough sleep because of my insomnia and having to wake up early for work everyday at 4 am, and I am currently in a stressful financial situation since I am moving to a new apartment in a couple days and I am very stressed.

Now, today I had what I am pretty sure was a hallucination or something else, I am not too sure, although all I know is that is felt TOO real.

Anyways, to start off, a couple of days ago at work, I accidentally stepped on a baby gecko as it ran under my shoe, It was a total accident and the gecko was injured in the head and could barely move, so I crushed it with my boot as fast and quickly as I could to end its suffering, which i felt was the right thing to do at the time, but idk.

Anyways, today, I was walking on a concrete path at work when yet another baby gecko ran out in front of me, but this time, i didnt try to steer away from it, but rather I brutally stomped it to death, crushing it and smearing its guts everywhere. Now before you go hating on me for being a cruel individual or a psychopath, hear me out...

I dont think that this actually happened and it could be a hallucination brought on from all my mental and emotional stress as well as the gecko incident from two days prior. The reason I am conflicted as to whether this incident was reality or a priduct of my imagination is that I am not that kind of a person, I would NEVER intentionally harm or kill an animal, especially a baby animal, out of malice or for pleasure. So after I was done killing the gecko, I began walking away and came to my senses. Why would I do that?! I thought to myself. So i quickly turned around to look again at the body of the gecko I had just brutally murdered but it was gone, in fact there was not a single trace of any dead gecko, no body, no blood, no stains, nothing, the whole scene just disappeared. And then after a couple of seconds, I notice a baby gecko run out from under a piece of trash and into a dirt field. How could the smeared body of a gecko just disappear without a trace? And where did the other gecko come from? Was me murdering the gecko only a product of my twisted mind? Because surely I know myself and I would never do that, nor had I ever thought about killing an animal.

Now even though I dont know if what I did was real or not, although it certaintly felt real, TOO real, I am very troubled at the thought of me actually doing this if it was real, and also the potential possibility of actually harming an animal while under some sort of psychotic episode. Does this sound like it could be a hallucination?

Progress, but feeling upset

I was struggling a TON February to May of last year, but I’m back to my baseline mental health, maybe even better than before because of all the skills I learned through the months. 

I’ve been progressing so much, but there’s one thing that has been hard for me to do: unpacking my psych ward bags. I was admitted to inpatient from PHP and spent 8 days there in late February, followed by more PHP where I finally discharged, but after a week and a half I was readmitted to PHP and then I spent almost the entirety of April in inpatient. 

It was my whole life for the entirety of April, so after I was discharged I kept everything packed, “Just incase.” 

I didn’t go back. Summer came and went and there’s still full paper bags of stuff sitting in my closet and the thought of throwing my coloring pages away, throwing my origami away, storing my notebooks, cleaning my clothes. It’s hard.

I can’t even decide why. I don’t want to go back, but part of me thinks that maybe I don’t want to because it feels like admitting that I’m better now. Mental illness was and is a HUGE part of my life and why I am how I am, and I’m not fully recovered. I don’t think I ever will be. But I’m BETTER. I’m in immediate danger, I’m not on a cocktail of medication anymore, I’m don’t need constant watch anymore and I think that’s scaring me. It’s just so hard, I’m sitting on my floor and dreading the task I’ve been procrastinating for so long.

Wish me luck, and thank you if you read all this. I truly love all of you, and I hope you are able to keep improving. Good luck <3

I became like them

When I was a child, I used to see my father lock himself in a dark room and not talk to anyone and remain weak for months. I used to see my mother crying every night because of this and asking his family for help every day. When I asked her what was happening to him, she said that he was sick, but I never believed it because he did not seem physically sick. Seeing my rough father become so weak and his voice disappear from the house as if he did not exist was confusing to me because my father usually talks a lot and screams for the most trivial reasons. My mother always asked us to be quiet and not to enter his room during those periods. Over the years, the desire to know why this happened to my father remained stuck in my head, but I finally understood what was happening to him because I was diagnosed with the same psychological disorder, obsessive-compulsive disorder. At the beginning of my infection, I did not understand what was happening. I thought that I had gone crazy and I used to see my father from afar looking at me with looks of pity because he knew very well what was wrong with me, but he did not try to explain to me even once what was happening to me or to tell me at least something to reassure me. He would only say that I needed medication as soon as possible so that  My mind calmed down but my mother was completely against the idea. She used to tell him that I was not crazy to need medicine. I was dying every second and my father knew very well what I was going through but he left me alone to suffer and he was living his life very normally while I was burning in hell and wishing for death. I hate my father for this. It is not enough that he inherited this curse for me and now his relatives leave me alone. He did not even think of taking me to a doctor. I lost several kilograms and I was not sleeping or eating in front of his eyes but he did not even think of saying a kind word to me to help me in my war and my mother did not understand me at all. She thought that I was doing this because I wanted attention and every time she would tell me to just ignore my thoughts because she never realized that the sound of my thoughts was so loud that even if I closed my ears I could hear it too

I adopted many bad habits that I have let go recently, and now I see all the damage I’ve done to myself and others. It was like I couldn’t see it before - I might’ve been too depressed or medicated, or else justifying my actions one way or another. My parents and siblings were all concerned throughout the years and urged me to try being better but I just kept doing me, thinking it was all going to be fine. Experimenting with certain drugs, hooking up with strangers, spending money when I should’ve been saving - It was getting me through university without me going insane, and this is me becoming an adult, is how I viewed it.

Earlier this year, with my well-meaning mother’s advice, I cut my SSRI cold turkey and fell into a manic/psychotic period that led into an existential crisis. Now, I am able to recognize the horrors - big spending spree that no one could talk me out of it and just spewing hateful words to certain siblings. And now, I am eaten up with regret, guilt, feelings of failure, and a depression that has never been so terrifying. I expressed my remorse to those I’ve hurt.

I don’t know if it’s what some call derealization or depersonalization. Sometimes get a deja vu feeling. I feel I do not belong anywhere in society and never will, and that I will just cause more undue suffering to myself and others. I started to freak out so I spoke a little louder and nobody moved, I got up from my desk and started repeating “no no what the fuck what’s going on” and when I stood up my vision was covered by these black figures, in hindsight I think i was really lighted headed and my vision was going in and out but in the moment and for months after I was convinced these were entities and in the moment they took the shape of people and they were everywhere. Then I blinked and everybody was staring at me so I moved to the front of the class asked for a note, and left the class, everybody at this point was staring at me like I was crazy. As I was walking to the nurses still in the most panicked state I’ve ever been the bell rung and I panicked even more. I walked through the staff entrance and immediately asked for the nurses attention, credit where it is due they helped me very very kindly. 

I sat in the nurses chair for a bit and described what had been happening but as I was sitting there after they questioned me a bunch still ina very petrified state I looked at them and asked a question. The same thing happened as it did the in the classroom and they didn’t move, nobody moved a muscle I got up and went to the desk but I kept my distance and they still didn’t look, I was so scared I started to yell. I ran into this small room where they held snacks and I sat down in the corner and was staring at the door, muttering, I was so scared of these people that I couldn’t deal with them coming into the room so i backed as far 
Agasint the wall as possible. I don’t remember coming out of this room but the next thing I do is the principal, nurses, and school counselor surrounding me in the chair I was previously at, I couldn’t deal with this either, panicked again and headed for the door, I went outside and the school counselor followed me. 

When I was outside he began to speak to me and I couldn’t hold it in, everything that I had been struggling with came out, I admitted to thinking i had BPD (I’m not diagnosed I’ve just done a lot of personal research and am scared a diagnosis will fuck up my life) and I explained what it was and why I think I had it. And he stared at me like I was stupid and he said made me feel ignorant about it all. I moved on from that and started ranting about pride and undiagnosed autism. This went on for a while and I just talked to him, eventually I calmed down but when I did I was in this state of being where I wasn’t in my body and everything felt like a dream, but I felt super calculated and extremely smart. We brought me back inside and he sat me in one of those beds that people sit who are sick at school lay in, but during our conversation I had drastic mood swings and would change my subject constantly, I started talking about how I loved my ex but didn’t want to date her because she wasn’t elevated to my level of being, my counselor reinforced this idea a few times during this experience, talking about how I was chosen for something and it tripped me out and i still despise myself for these moments and am petrified of being a narcissist because of them. Eventually I was brought to the Principals and then home, my dad and mom were so mad my dad almost resorted back to old punishments, and claimed I was either gonna end up dead or in prison like my uncles. I didn’t feel like myself for the coming weeks and I wrote in this journal for hours and hours, previously I had never journaled but I wrote these passages about commandments and how to elevate yourself and become the person you were meant to by, I still have the pages if anybody would like to see them, they’re crazy and I think I destroyed a few of them because my sister tried to open the book. I know I can’t ever go back to being who I was before this experience. 

I’ve never told anybody this complete story and if you made it this far thank you for taking the time to read about my experience. At this point in my life I’m pretty sure I have BPD after researching hyper specific symptoms I have, the results on google, Reddit, and personal conversation all lead me to believe I have BPD but I know a self diagnosis can be a dangerous thing which is why I still do not make the claim I have BPD, I am just leaning towards that claim. I feel like I’m splitting as of right now and I’m trying not to give in to the thoughts about relapse But that could realistically just be in my head.

I don't experience the normal system stuff. And I'm not here for a diagnosis on any specific disorder. Or a diagnosis at all. I just need to vent a little bit because I will blow up otherwise. 

When I was 12, I was facing abuse from a family member. And I'm like 96% sure because of that abuse, an alter formed. I called her Anti and she wasn't kind to me at all at first, quite the opposite. A few months after this, I decided to make another being inside my head to "protect" me from her and I called this one Angel. After a while of thinking for Angel, he began thinking on his own. And soon after Anti changed and is now very protective of me, and constantly apologizes for what she did. Even though I don't hate her for it lol. In like, March-April of last year, I went thru a stressful situation and began hearing another voice. From what I know, Angel and Anti tried to hide him because they were scared of my reaction, since I was terrified of forming a ton of alters and losing myself within them. But it's only been them so far, and I named him Uni, and he also protects me. So including me, there's 4 of us. 

The thing is that, as far as I know, I don't have the normal experiences of somebody in a system. I can't remember times if I was possibly Angel or Anti before realizing this, but considering the timeline of events from Anti forming I don't doubt me being a system. But I was already so old by then. There's a slim chance she formed sooner and was just dormant up until I was 12, but like I said that's a slim chance since I can't recall any sort of evidence like that. I'm like 99% sure I have ADHD, so a lot of symptoms that could be bc of a disorder, could also trace back to the very possible ADHD. Trying to have them front was hard, not because it was hard to trigger them forward, but because I was terrified of the idea of not being me and giving up control of my body. Although we figured it out and have boundaries related to fronting to avoid me being uncomfortable. Sometimes we switch without knowing either but there's no indicators. It's just a realization of "oh no wait I'm actually this alter." 

The thing that also puts me off is that I only started being aware and experiencing this stuff AFTER I began identifying as a system. So now I'm going thru a crisis wondering if my friend just told me I was, and accidentally deluded me into thinking I was a system (they are one too btw) but I don't have any evidence this isnt the first time I've experienced any of this. I can faintly recall moments where I just wasn't myself when I was younger. But that's kind of it. I can't actually think of any times before this where I "wasn't me." We have good communication, there's no weird gaps in my memory that isn't ADHD related (possibly). Angel was the first to front and it did REALLY feel like I was him, and not me. And I don't connect to others in system spaces or their experiences. I feel so awkward because on discord servers, they have like 20+ alters, joke about it, have fictives and introjects from media they like, and constantly experience symptoms I've never felt before. And I'm over here rarely having my alters front, never talking about it, and literally just not presenting as somebody who is multiple. The ONLY reason I think it's a possibility at this point is the timeline that Anti and Uni formed. I created Angel, I know that. But it doesn't make him feel any less of an alter like the others. 

It's terrifying to think that none of this was actually real and I've been just lying to myself on accident for months. It's nearly been a year at this point since I came to this realization. Was there some sort of fronting barrier I had to break down? Because I remember trying to get them to front was super hard, and it was terrifying. I have experienced fusion before. Not my alters tho, ME specifically. And I have no idea who I even fused with! My mind just started going dark and I couldn't focus on anything else other than what was going on. It took me like 30 minutes to properly ground myself, and it was one of the most anxiety inducing experiences of my life. Because I was terrified that I would go dormant, and I'm convinced I almost did. 

Anyway, I just seriously needed to vent about this because going into system spaces is so awkward. Especially to ask if I am a system, and then have an alter that isn't even born from fucking trauma. I'm so confused, and I am gonna cry.

To explain: always been absurdly obsessive about appearances. As in, I cannot mentally cope with or align with atypical thinking on appearances. It drives me up the fucking wall. I often get irrationally angry over the sheer thought of ugly people existing, and I have panic attacks when I try and do things that my mind has decided to irrational consider as being "infected by the presense of ugly people". This ranges from basically everything in my life, including fundamental things like eating, and even sleeping (I'm hugely insomnactic due to this reason). For most of my life I've mistaken my issues for some sort of extreme form of a cacophobia disorder, but in recent years I've come to understand my issues more "clearly" (or to be accurate, what it ISN'T more clearly), although that's only made them worse. 

I think the worst part about my problems have to be these two things, though:

1. Nothing identifiable--not really having any semblance of logic or consistency--not even in a way that is non-neurotypical, yet still has some thread of consistency to it
2. People just not believing this is a real thing, because I cannot explain it that clearly
3. The absolute ridicule I get from all sides, because everyone thinks I'm just a narcissist with a made up mental illness

Really, I want to say that "the actual issues I have isn't the worst part, it's the fact that no one listens to them or cares about them", but those two things have just blurred together now and I cannot even tell them apart. No one takes me the least bit seriously, and the dissonance between how insincerely people always treat my problems, and how sincerely suffocating they feel, is driving me to the brink. I've begun to sincerely limit every form of contact I have, and I'v got nothing left in my life.

I'm at the end of my rope here. I turn 30 in around a week, and I've always promised myself I'd end my life before I turn 30, since I hate the idea of getting older and "uglier"--but even that's a scary prospect for me, and as I've been getting closer to the date, I've just gone back on it, because I can't stop thinking stuff like "what if there IS something after death", and I also worry about what happens to my body when I'm gone.

Can anyone explain why I can’t touch napkins?

I’m gonna start this off by saying that none of my diagnoses explicitly explain this aversion. I’m 20afab, diagnosed with ADHD, with high indications of anxiety and depression, and for some inexplicable reason I have a huge aversion towards any thin paper material. This includes napkins, tissues, tissue paper, kitchen roll, and toilet paper (but I’ve found a sort of way around that part for obviously necessary reasons). Seeing people use them is so disturbing to me and I am always hyper aware of if there is a balled up or wrinkled tissue in my line of sight. I can’t take my eyes off it, and I will do anything to get it away without touching it. It’s worse if they’re wet, used, or balled up, but even a clean one can’t get too close to my face without triggering a sort of quiet panic. I don’t think I’ve used a tissue to blow my nose since I was a very young child, and when I was little I would scream if my siblings teased me by throwing them in my direction. I will always make sure I know if there’s a sink to wash my hands in before getting them dirty because I dread having to use a napkin. A wet napkin is one of the most repulsive things I can think of. I can (and have) used kitchen rolls and napkins to clean spills or messes, but as soon as they’re “tainted” in my eyes I can’t think of anything other than how to get away from them as soon as possible. I have never met someone with this kind of aversion, and I have never met someone who’s been able to relate on any level. If I accidentally touch one, or a tainted napkin has touched any of my belongings, I struggle to set that uneasiness aside for what could be hours. If a napkin touches my food, I feel nauseous at the thought of eating the part it touched.
